findings: Feature: new `keel dashboard` CLI sub-command in bin/keel.js — serves a local HTTP server (default port 7772).
The dashboard is strictly read-only; no write actions are exposed.
Data source: .keel/state/ directory — each story sub-directory contains a manifest.json with story ID, title, scope, phase, status, and updated_at.
Phase display must use phase name (not phase number) — names must map from the numeric phase stored in manifest.
Status values to display: COMPLETE, IN PROGRESS, HALTED — these must derive from manifest status fields.
Idle time calculation follows the established convention (ADR/conventions.md 2026-07-14): Xh Ym when >= 60 min; Xm Ys when < 60 min; absent timestamp shows 'unknown'.
Sort order: stories sorted by updated_at descending (most recently active first).
Auto-refresh: page must refresh every 30 seconds via meta refresh or JS — no server-side push required.
Port override: --port flag on CLI overrides default 7772; only AC-5 requires this flag to be supported.
Empty state: if .keel/state/ directory is absent, the page must show 'No stories found. Run keel init <story-id> to start.' — server still starts and serves this message.
Zero new npm dependencies — implementation restricted to Node.js built-ins: http, fs, path, child_process.
HTML is generated inline within the JS source — no external template files, no bundler.
AC-7 is a regression guard: existing `status --all` output from keel-state.cjs must remain byte-for-byte unchanged.
Server startup confirmation: stdout must print exactly 'Dashboard: http://localhost:<port>' on start.
Imported from KEEL-104 story brief provided by human PO — not authored by BA agent.
decisions: Imported from KEEL-104 story brief provided by human PO, not authored by BA agent.
Idle time display follows project convention established 2026-07-14 (KEEL-103) in conventions.md.
No open questions raised — brief is complete and testable as written; all 7 ACs are unambiguous.
